This stage is the app server’s end-to-end lifecycle test layer for conversational execution. It sits across the main request/response loop and persistence boundary, proving that threads, turns, reviews, and session state behave correctly as clients create work, mutate it, persist it, reload it, and receive notifications.

The thread-focused suites define the contract for starting, listing, loading, reading, resuming, archiving, unarchiving, deleting, forking, rolling back, unsubscribing, and summarizing conversations, including rollout-backed storage, SQLite state, ephemeral sessions, parent/child relationships, and remote thread-store routing. Settings and metadata tests verify per-thread memory mode, settings, metadata patches, names, loaded-thread reporting, and memory reset, along with websocket broadcasts and state repair.

Execution suites cover turn start, interrupt, steer, injected items, output schemas, dynamic tools, plan extraction, permission and user-input tool calls, and client metadata propagation into downstream model requests. Review and compaction tests validate secondary execution flows that operate on existing threads. Finally, status and safety-notification suites ensure active sessions emit the right policy, reroute, moderation, and runtime status events, while external-agent import tests verify migrated histories integrate cleanly into the same lifecycle.
